description:
You are the database administrator of a small SaaS company. Two existing INI files under /home/user/db_configs/ hold the current connection parameters and a catalog of SQL statements. Your job is to (a) tweak one production-side parameter and (b) generate a concise report of the statements that are explicitly marked as slow. IMPORTANT: 1. Do NOT hard-code any values; they must be read from the INI files. 2. The automated checker will verify both the edited INI file and the newly created report word-for-word. 3. Create a log file as specified so that the checker can validate the result. 4. The whole task can be completed with roughly 5–10 normal shell commands (grep, awk, sed, etc.). Root privileges are NOT available. Initial file locations • /home/user/db_configs/connection.ini • /home/user/db_configs/queries.ini Required end-state A. INI update • In /home/user/db_configs/connection.ini, inside the section [production], change the value of the key pool_size to “30”. • Leave every other line in the file untouched (order, spacing and comments must stay exactly as they were). B. Report generation • Make sure the directory /home/user/reports/ exists; create it if it does not. • Produce the file /home/user/reports/slow_queries_report.log in the format shown below. • Only SQL statements whose section in queries.ini contains the key/value pair slow=yes should be included. • They must be sorted by their numerical priority value in ascending order (lowest number first). • Report format (exactly, including spacing): 
=== Slow Queries (sorted by priority asc) ===
<rank>) <section_name> | priority=<priority_number> | <query_text>
– <rank> begins with 1 and increments by 1 for every line. – There are **no blank lines** before, after or between report lines. – Make sure there are no trailing spaces at line endings. Example of a correctly formatted two-line report (for illustration only): 
=== Slow Queries (sorted by priority asc) ===
1) some_query | priority=1 | SELECT ...
2) another_one | priority=3 | SELECT ...
C. Console confirmation • After completing steps A and B, print exactly this message to STDOUT: Optimization settings updated. Report generated at /home/user/reports/slow_queries_report.log • The message is for human visibility; the automated tester will judge only the files but it is good practice to emit it. Hints (you don’t have to follow them, they’re just guidance) • To update the single key in connection.ini without altering formatting, consider using sed -i with an anchored regex inside the production section. • To parse INI files, typical solutions involve awk or grep + cut. • mkdir -p can safely create the reporting directory even if it already exists. Once you have satisfied all the requirements, feel free to double-check your work by viewing the files. The grader will run automated checks to ensure: 1. /home/user/db_configs/connection.ini contains the new pool_size=30 line only within the [production] section. 2. /home/user/reports/slow_queries_report.log exists and its contents exactly match the specification. 3. No extra files were created inside /home/user/db_configs/ or /home/user/reports/. Good luck!
truth:
Initial file system state (already present before the agent starts):

1. /home/user/db_configs/connection.ini
-------------------------------------------------
[development]
host=localhost
port=5432
user=dev
password=devpass
pool_size=5

[staging]
host=staging.db.internal
port=5432
user=staging
password=stagingpass
pool_size=10

[production]
host=prod.db.internal
port=5432
user=prod
password=prodpass
pool_size=20
-------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/db_configs/queries.ini
-------------------------------------------------
[number_users]
query=SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;
slow=no
priority=3

[big_join]
query=SELECT u.id, o.id FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id=o.user_id;
slow=yes
priority=2

[monthly_report]
query=SELECT * FROM reports WHERE month=CURRENT_DATE;
slow=yes
priority=5

[tmp_cleanup]
query=DELETE FROM temp WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day';
slow=no
priority=1

[yearly_stats]
query=SELECT * FROM stats_yearly;
slow=yes
priority=4
-------------------------------------------------

3. No directory called /home/user/reports/ is present at start-up.

Expected final artifacts after correct task execution:

A. /home/user/db_configs/connection.ini  
The file must be identical to the original except that **only** the pool_size line inside the [production] block reads:
pool_size=30

B. /home/user/reports/slow_queries_report.log  
Exact required content:
-------------------------------------------------
=== Slow Queries (sorted by priority asc) ===
1) big_join | priority=2 | SELECT u.id, o.id FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id=o.user_id;
2) yearly_stats | priority=4 | SELECT * FROM stats_yearly;
3) monthly_report | priority=5 | SELECT * FROM reports WHERE month=CURRENT_DATE;
-------------------------------------------------

No extra lines, spaces or carriage returns.

C. The directory /home/user/reports/ exists (permission default 755) and contains only the above log file unless the shell or editor used by the agent leaves harmless temporary files (those will be ignored by the grader).

The automated tests will:
• Use diff to compare connection.ini with a golden copy that has pool_size=30 in [production] but is otherwise unchanged.
• Compare slow_queries_report.log byte-for-byte with the expected block above.
